data.title: Same Entity Unstakes 2.92 Million HYPE After Eight Months
data.description: Two wallets belonging to the same entity unstaked 2,920,000 HYPE after eight months of staking, according to Onchain Lens. According to Odaily, the two wallets now hold 0 HYPE in the staking contract.

data.title: Ritholtz's Josh Brown says investors should rotate out of Magnificent 7 into broader AI plays
data.description: According to CNBC, Ritholtz Management co-founder and CEO Josh Brown said investors should shift in the second half of this year out of the Magnificent 7 and other hyperscalers and into a wider group of stocks as part of a broadening-out trade. Brown said non-hyperscalers showing business improvement from AI-related upside earnings surprises are the kind of names investors should focus on. He cited Travelers Companies and Chubb Limited as insurers that fit the theme, and he also named Caterpillar and GE Vernova as industrials that offer exposure to the trend. Brown's comments followed Alphabet's Wednesday increase to its 2024 capital expenditures forecast to between $195 billion and $205 billion, a move that triggered selling in hyperscaler stocks on Thursday, including Meta Platforms, Microsoft and Amazon. Travelers and Chubb were both up between 1% and 2% Thursday, while Caterpillar rose about 1% and GE Vernova gained 5%.

data.title: Options traders watch S&P 500 gamma levels as index faces pressure
data.description:
According to CNBC, crude oil is rallying, bonds are selling off, and the stock market is under pressure after investors dumped Big Tech shares following earnings, while the 10-year Treasury yield touched 4.7%, its highest level since January 2025. The S&P 500 is still less than 3% below its record, but options traders are tracking whether trading around key index levels could signal more volatility or a deeper pullback.

Evidence from SpotGamma, Barchart and Cboe LiveVol suggested market makers were likely long gamma for at least a month into this week, with the largest positions concentrated around 7,500 in the S&P 500. That has helped keep the index mostly within a 200-point range since mid-May, though Barchart's volatility model put the flip point at 7,500 and said the State Street SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) would face higher sell-off risk if it fell below 740. Barchart options product manager Brendan Herbert said the market is in a negative gamma regime and that market makers may have to sell to hedge if the market drops. SpotGamma founder Brent Kochuba said positive gamma has eased but remains fairly light through 7,300, and he said he would add short-dated, cheap, out-of-the-money put flies with a bearish bias.

data.title: STOCKS | Hut 8, CleanSpark, and MARA Rise as Bitcoin Falls and IREN Signs $2.8 Billion AI Cloud Contracts
data.description:
Top U.S. Bitcoin miners Hut 8, CleanSpark, and MARA rose 3% to 7% Thursday even as Bitcoin fell about 2%. According to NS3.AI, Hut 8 also signed a second 15-year lease for 352 megawatts of IT capacity at its Beacon Point campus.

IREN Limited separately signed $2.8 billion in new AI cloud contracts. The agreements add to activity across the mining and AI infrastructure sectors as companies expand capacity and contract pipelines.

data.description: AMD CEO Lisa Su said OpenAI is making a big bet on AMD. According to Jin10, Su made the remark about OpenAI and AMD.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Super Micro Launches New Server Portfolio With AMD EPYC 9006 Series
data.description: According to Jin10, Super Micro introduced a new server product portfolio powered by sixth-generation AMD EPYC 9006 series processors, delivering 1.7 times generational performance improvement.

data.title: Progressive Groups Target Gillibrand In Crypto Bill Fight
data.description: According to Axios, progressive groups including Indivisible, Demand Progress and the Revolving Door Project are targeting Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand as she works to broker a compromise on the CLARITY Act, which still has unresolved ethics provisions. The groups said Gillibrand, who chairs the Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee, is undermining efforts to hold the Trump administration accountable for corruption, and argued that her son's involvement in an equity trading platform leaves Democrats vulnerable to the same criticism. The CLARITY Act still needs 60 votes in the Senate this summer, and Republican Sens. Bernie Moreno and Cynthia Lummis are working with the White House on compromise language while Sen. Thom Tillis leads separate bipartisan talks on the ethics section. The crypto industry’s flagship super PAC, Fairshake, has about $125 million on hand, and the bill cleared the Senate Banking Committee in May with support from Democrats Ruben Gallego and Angela Alsobrooks. Axios also noted that last year's GENIUS Act, which created the first federal framework for dollar-backed stablecoins, prompted tense Democratic debate and ultimately won support from 18 Senate Democrats.

data.title: U.S. Central Command Redirects 12 Commercial Ships and Disables 1 Vessel in 9 Days
data.description: U.S. Central Command said it has redirected 12 commercial ships to change course and rendered one vessel incapable of operating in the nine days since maritime blockades on Iran were resumed. According to Jin10, the command gave the update without further detail.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Shanghai Gold Falls 2.25% as Shanghai Silver Drops 4.22%
data.description: At 2:30, the main Shanghai gold contract closed down 2.25% at 884 yuan per gram, and the main Shanghai silver contract closed down 4.22% at 14,110 yuan per kilogram. According to Jin10, the SC crude oil main contract closed up 7.11% at 604 yuan per barrel.

data.title: STOCKS | U.S. Crude Futures Close at $92.19, Up 6.17%
data.description: U.S. crude futures settled at $92.19 per barrel, up $5.36, or 6.17%. According to Jin10, that was the settlement price.

data.title: Alphabet falls more than 6% as oil surge lifts yields
data.description:
According to CNBC, stocks pulled back Thursday as rising oil prices pushed the U.S. benchmark WTI crude back above $90 a barrel, international benchmark Brent above $100 a barrel and the 10-year Treasury yield to 4.70%, its highest level since January 2025. The market now sees about an 83% chance the Federal Reserve will hike rates in September, up from 53% a week ago, according to the CME FedWatch tool.

Alphabet dropped more than 6% and was on pace for its worst day of the year after it reported negative free cash flow in the second quarter and raised its full-year capital expenditure outlook. Eli Lilly said retatrutide produced positive results in two late-stage trials, and the company plans to submit the obesity drug for approval to the Food and Drug Administration in the first quarter of 2027 through a Biologics License Application. Intel is due to report after the closing bell, followed Friday morning by earnings from American Express, Verizon, Charter Communications, SLB and HCA Healthcare.

data.title: ECB Considers Raising Reserve Requirements to Ease Loss Pressure
data.description: The European Central Bank is considering several options to ease pressure from its financial losses, and the related discussions are expected to reach a key stage this autumn. According to Jin10, ECB President Christine Lagarde said policymakers will discuss raising minimum reserve requirements, which would increase the share of commercial banks' funds held in the central bank's non-interest-bearing accounts. The ECB is also evaluating a tiered interest-rate mechanism that would stop paying interest on some excess reserves, as well as charging banks fees. These measures are intended to reduce the burden on national central banks and offset part of the losses from stimulus policies over the past decade. The euro system has faced loss pressure after the ECB carried out a large-scale bond-buying program from 2015 to 2022 and paid banks substantial interest after rapid rate hikes from 2022 to 2023. According to Jin10, sources said there are still major differences over this politically sensitive issue.

data.title: STOCKS | Miami to Sell Griffin Historic Site Across From Planned Citadel HQ
data.description:
Miami has agreed to sell Ken Griffin a site across the street from Citadel’s planned headquarters in the Brickell financial district, completing his goal of owning the entire square block, according to Bloomberg.

The parcel currently houses an historic cottage.

data.title: Tassat Unveils Project NENYA for Stablecoin Reserve Management
data.description:
Tassat unveiled Project NENYA, a stablecoin reserve management platform designed for regional and midsize U.S. banks, as the sector is projected to grow into a multi-trillion-dollar market. According to NS3.AI, the platform is expected to help regulated stablecoin issuers allocate reserves across banks and tokenized high-quality liquid assets.

The company said pilot programs are set to begin in the first half of the year, with a full launch planned for early 2027. Tassat said the platform is aimed at giving banks and issuers a regulated framework for managing stablecoin reserves.

data.title: Hyperliquid Generates $1.19 Million in Fees and Burns 17,600 HYPE
data.description: Onchain Lens posted on X that Hyperliquid generated $1.19 million in fee revenue today and burned 17,600 HYPE tokens worth $1.03 million. According to Odaily, the post said the tokens were destroyed as part of the activity reported for the day.
